import io
import string
import list

fn parse_ints(_ s: String) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  let n = string.len(s)
  mut i = 0
  mut current = ""
  mut has = false
  while i < n
    match string.byte_at(s, i)
    when Some(b)
      if b == 32 or b == 9 or b == 10 or b == 13
        if has
          match string.parse_int(current)
          when Some(v)
            result = list.push(result, v)
          when None
            ()
          end
          current = ""
          has = false
        end
      else
        current = "#{current}#{string.from_byte(b)}"
        has = true
      end
    when None
      ()
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  if has
    match string.parse_int(current)
    when Some(v)
      result = list.push(result, v)
    when None
      ()
    end
  end
  result
end

fn render_reversed(_ nums: List<Int>) -> String
  let n = nums.len()
  mut out = ""
  mut first = true
  mut i = n - 1
  while i >= 0
    match nums.get(i)
    when Some(v)
      if first
        out = "#{v}"
        first = false
      else
        out = "#{out} #{v}"
      end
    when None
      ()
    end
    i = i - 1
  end
  out
end

match io.read_line()
when Some(line)
  let nums = parse_ints(line)
  println(render_reversed(nums))
when None
  println("")
end
